title: ricinus communis subvar. gracilis
intro: Ricinus communis subvar. gracilis, commonly known as the castor bean or ricinus communis gracilis, is a variant of the Ricinus communis plant, a member of the spurge family. This plant is primarily used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The ricinus communis gracilis plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, improving skin and hair health, and supporting immune function. Castor oil, extracted from the seeds of the plant, is often prepared through cold-pressing and is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as massage oils, creams, and capsules, due to its medicinal properties.
